title: Business Valuation Services Bangalore | Virtual Auditor
description: Expert business valuation in Bangalore. IBBI registered valuer. DCF, market multiples, NAV methods. SEBI/FEMA/Companies Act compliant.
bodyText:
Looking for business valuation services in Bangalore? Virtual Auditor provides IBBI-compliant business valuations in Bangalore, led by CA V. Viswanathan — Fellow Chartered Accountant (FCA), IBBI Registered Valuer (Reg. No. IBBI/RV/03/2019/12333), and Associate Company Secretary (ACS). We deliver valuation reports accepted by regulators, investors, and courts.

What is Business Valuation Services Bangalore | Virtual Auditor?

  HEADING2: What We Deliver
  IBBI-compliant valuation report for regulatory filings (Companies Act Section 247, FEMA, SEBI, Income Tax). DCF (Discounted Cash Flow) valuation with detailed financial projections and WACC computation. Market multiples analysis using comparable company and transaction data. Net Asset Value (NAV) method for asset-heavy businesses. Fair Market Value determination for share transfers, M&A, and tax compliance. Valuation certificate for bank loans, investor rounds, and NCLT proceedings. Expert opinion for dispute resolution and arbitration.
  HEADING2: Why Choose Virtual Auditor for Business Valuation in Bangalore
  Bangalore's startup and corporate ecosystem requires valuations for multiple purposes — investor fundraising, ESOP grants, share transfers, merger schemes, and regulatory compliance. Each purpose demands a different valuation approach and regulatory standard. CA V. Viswanathan (IBBI Registered Valuer) brings practitioner-level expertise across all valuation standards — ICAI Valuation Standards, IVS (International Valuation Standards), and IBBI rules. Our valuations are accepted by ROC, NCLT, Income Tax authorities, RBI (for FEMA), and SEBI. Our MG Road office provides in-person meetings for complex valuation engagements.
  HEADING2: Our Process
  Step 1: Engagement scoping — purpose, regulatory requirement, timeline, and deliverable format. Step 2: Data collection — financials, projections, industry data, comparable transactions. Step 3: Valuation analysis — multiple methods applied (DCF, market multiples, NAV). Step 4: Draft report with valuation range and recommended fair value. Step 5: Management discussion and incorporation of feedback. Step 6: Final IBBI-compliant valuation report with certificate. Step 7: Support during regulatory filing or investor negotiation.
  HEADING2: Get Started Today
  Need a business valuation in Bangalore? Contact us:
  Call/WhatsApp: +91-95139 39333
  Email: support@virtualauditor.in
  Visit: 7th Floor, Mahalakshmi Chambers, 29, MG Road, Bangalore 560001

faqs.q: What qualifications does your valuer hold?
faqs.a: CA V. Viswanathan is an IBBI Registered Valuer (Securities or Financial Assets, Reg. No. IBBI/RV/03/2019/12333), Fellow Chartered Accountant (FCA), and Associate Company Secretary (ACS). IBBI registration is mandatory for valuations under Companies Act Section 247 and IBC.

faqs.q: How long does a business valuation take?
faqs.a: Typically 7-15 working days depending on complexity, data availability, and purpose. Urgent valuations for regulatory deadlines can be delivered in 3-5 days with premium pricing.

faqs.q: What valuation methods do you use?
faqs.a: We apply multiple methods — DCF (income approach), comparable company multiples (market approach), and NAV (asset approach) — and triangulate to arrive at fair value. The method weightage depends on the business type, stage, and valuation purpose.
